visual_description: A medium close-up shot focuses on a man and a woman sitting at a table, engaged in conversation. The man, on the right, has dark, wavy hair and a beard. He wears a striped sweater and holds a sandwich in his hands, gesturing with it as he speaks. The woman, on the left, is seen from behind, showing her wavy, shoulder-length blonde hair and a dark ribbed sweater. The background is a bustling, out-of-focus public space, likely a cafeteria or food court, filled with other people. The camera angle shifts slightly, revealing the woman's face. She has a concerned expression and is also holding a sandwich. A woman speaks with a direct, slightly challenging tone, asking, "How do you know?" The man continues to look at her, his expression serious as he continues to talk and hold his food. The camera remains steady, maintaining the focus on their interaction. A man responds, his voice sounding a bit weary and questioning as he repeats her words, "You mean, how do I know I know?" The background is filled with the low, ambient murmur of a busy public space, suggesting a cafeteria or a crowded restaurant. There are no on-screen graphics.
